title: Enflame Wins IPO Approval for 6 Billion Yuan AI Chip Listing
content:
This article first appeared on GuruFocus.

Tencent (TCEHY)-backed Shanghai Enflame Technology has moved closer to a major public-market debut after receiving approval for an initial public offering on Shanghai's STAR board. The company, viewed as the last of China's four little dragons of artificial intelligence chipmakers to come to market, plans to raise 6 billion yuan, or about $888 million, by selling between 10% and 15% of its shares. Enflame expects to use the proceeds to support the development and production of AI cloud chips and related software, positioning itself deeper inside China's fast-growing AI infrastructure race.

Warning! GuruFocus has detected 4 Warning Sign with LAZ. Is TCEHY fairly valued? Test your thesis with our free DCF calculator.

Investor interest could be strong, given the recent wave of Chinese technology listings tied to the artificial intelligence supply chain. Enflame follows peers including Moore Threads Technology, Shanghai Biren Technology and MetaX Integrated Circuits Shanghai, with Moore Threads jumping 425% on its December debut. Tencent Holdings, which owns 20% of Enflame and remains a major customer, gives the company a powerful commercial base, as Tencent's internet and cloud platforms use Enflame's chips in large data centers for applications such as chat bots, recommendation systems and generative AI.

Still, the company's rapid growth comes with profitability questions. Sales to Tencent accounted for 84% of Enflame's total revenue in 2025, up from around 38% the previous year, while net losses narrowed to 1.2 billion yuan from 1.5 billion yuan. Revenue is expected to more than triple year-on-year to about 10.6 billion yuan to 11.5 billion yuan in the first half, while Enflame forecast a loss of about 600 million yuan for the same period. The company said Tencent's demand for AI accelerator cards has far exceeded its supply capacity, suggesting Enflame could remain a closely watched name as investors assess China's AI chip pipeline.
